_doc: Editor steering notes for the conference preview agent. Authoritative — these override prior knowledge and any conflicting context-data interpretation. Keep each note to 1–3 short sentences. Empty strings are dropped silently. Loaded by build_conference_context.py from /cfb-research/writer_notes/<season>/<conf>.json.
_usage: conference_note: 1–3 sentences with two purposes — (a) the meta-narrative for the year (e.g., 'The realignment dust has settled but the talent gap between the top three and the rest is still widening.'), and (b) calibration the agent needs to right-size confidence: what 'good' looks like in this league. SEC example: 'In modern SEC: 7-1 in conf typically reaches the title game; 9-0 has never happened. 9 overall wins is a historic season.' MAC example: 'In the MAC, 8 conference wins is championship caliber; 6-2 contends.' Combine narrative + calibration when both matter.

team_notes.<slug>: 1–3 sentences about that specific team — what the data won't tell the agent (a coaching change, a critical transfer, an injury, an off-field story, the angle the writeup should take).
Slugs match the canonical team slugs in build_team_context.py / scrape_team_context.py.
conference_note: The 2026 Mountain West conference has 10 members after 5 schools (Boise St, Colorado St, San Diego St, Fresno St and Utah St) left for the new Pac 12 conference.  The MWC added new FBS member North Dakota State, Northern Illinois from the MAC and UTEP from Conference USA.  Three storylines define the 2026 race: (1) Can anyone take Boise St's place as the annual favorite of this new MWC?  (2) Can brand new FBS member North Dakota State actually contend in this new MWC? (3) Outside of San Jose State winning the MWC title in the COVID shortened 2020 season, no current member of the conference has ever won the MWC championship. All G6 conference teams are dealing with the new portal era in their own way, but the reality is players that stand out at this level usually end up on a Power 4 roster the following season. MWC Tier framework: When team_notes give a 3-tier range (e.g., Tier 2-4), it's mainly due to a new look conference trying to sort out who are contenders and who are not. The agent should NOT over-anchor on a single 'centering' tier; instead treat the range itself as the projection. A team at Tier 2-4 means 'either it works and they're contending for the title game or it doesn't and they're fighting for a bowl' — both branches are real. Conference USA tier framework (tiers describe team caliber, NOT exact record): Tier 1 = MWC title-game favorite, lower tier CFB bowl game contenders ; Tier 2 = title-game contender, lower tier bowl game contenders; Tier 3 = middle tier and title-game and bowl game outsider; Tier 4 = bottom of the conference where reaching bowl eligibility would itself be a tremendous achievement. When team_notes give a range, the agent should treat the wider boundary as the realistic downside — but per the volatility note above, 3-tier ranges are common here and the range itself should be framed as the projection rather than collapsing to a single tier. The Mid-American Championship game is played in the home stadium of the team with the best overall conference win percentage.

team_notes.air-force: Tier 2-3 MWC Team: HC Troy Calhoun one of CFB's longest tenured coaches entering his 20th season.  QB Liam Szarka returns as starter, a good now running threat scored 13 TDs last season, was cleared from a forearm fracture suffered in November 2025.  RB Owen Allen returns, depth must emerge; OL returns 2 full time starters and 3 more with part time experience.  DL has limited experience but LBs have 4 starters back; secondary returns 4 starters from a unit that was #135 vs pass last season. Offense replaces 8 starters, defense returns 8 starters;
team_notes.hawaii: Tier 2 MWC Team: Hawaii jumped from 5 wins in 2024 to 9 wins in 2025, going 9-4 overall.  Hawaii is a very pass heavy team: 62% pass, 38% run.  QB Micah Alejado was the MWC Freshman of the Year in 2025 and returns for his 2nd year as the starter. WR Pofele Ashlock was 2nd team All MWC in 2025.  RB Cam Barfield returns, a productive back with good receiving skills; OL returns 3 starters from last season.  Defense replaces a lot and has been weak vs the run, but CB Elijah Palmer is a big time playmaker, as is DE Lesterlaisene Lagafuaina.
team_notes.nevada: Tier 3-4 MWC Team: Lowest projected power rated team in MWC in preseason.  Offense was bottom 10 in FBS last season.  QB Carter Jones returns after taking over as starter as a freshman last fall, but must improve.  Added two FCS transfers at WR to boost unit, RB Herschel Turner is back, along with both starting OT's but the rest of the OL is new.  Def lost a lot, but has DE Dylan LaBarbera, who was 1st team All MWC in 2025, and a good looking duo at CB in Joshua Scott and Aiden Walker .
team_notes.new-mexico: Tier 1 MWC Team: HC Jason Eck was the MWC coach of the year in 2025. LB Jaxton Eck is the reigning MWC Defensive Player of the Year. Projected to win 7.5 by Vegas, tied for 2nd highest win total in the MWC.  16 starters return, highest in the MWC.  QB Jack Layne returns for his 3rd season starting, although must improve a 13 TD/10 Int ratio; RB rebuild with Scottre Humphrey only one back, although 4 starting OL return; WR get a boost from two portal adds led by Troy Omeire from UNLV. Defense was stronger unit last year and projects to be again this season, led by LB Eck behind a good DL with players like DE Darren Agu & DT Brian Booker. CB Frankie Edwards III coming off a very productive Sophomore season.
team_notes.north-dakota-state: Tier 1-2 MWC Team: NDSU joins the FBS in 2026 after winning five of the last 10 FCS national championships. Projected to win 9.5 by Vegas, highest win total in the MWC; now eligible for postseason after new NCAA ruling allowing FCS transition teams to play in FBS postseason in their first season.  QB is new this year in Nathan Hayes who has just a few mop up roles under his belt; RB  DJ Scott was their #2 last season, OL returns 3 full time starters.  TE Reis Kessel a good one, one of best in MWC. Defense returns 6 starters led by good DL with DT Keenan Wilson and DE Kelton McCaslin leading the way, LB Donovan Woolen just behind them.  CB looks to be biggest question on defense, QB & WR are biggest questions on offense.
team_notes.northern-illinois: Tier 4 MWC Team: Projected to win 3.5 by Vegas, lowest win total in the MWC.  Left MAC conference to join MWC after last season.  Former HC Thomas Hammock left for NFL assistant gig, new HC is Rob Harley who was the Defensive Coordinator. Offense was awful last season and must rebuild, starting with new QB Jalen Macon who gives them a mobile threat.  RB  Telly Johnson Jr. looks like best weapon on team, although WR DeAree Rogers also productive; OL returns 2 starters and must improve.  Defense was the better unit last season but relying on a lot of FCS transfers to fill positions and the front seven seems to lack both size and FBS experience.  Program looks in really rough shape.
team_notes.san-jose-state: Tier 3-4 MWC Team: The last MWC champion still in this league, the Spartans won the title in the Covid-shortened 2020 season.  3 man race for starting QB job; OC Stutzmann runs a fast paced 'spread & shred' offense to protect the OL; Was a productive offense last season but not one skill player returning with over 100 yards rushing or receiving last season & the OL returns only 2 starters.  If this unit is going to success a lot of questions are going to require answers not found on paper.  Defense returns very little as well; Spartans have the lowest returning production metric of any team at just 35%, and that number includes portal players from former schools.
team_notes.unlv: Tier 1 MWC Team: Lost MWC title game to Boise last fall, good season with a 10-4 record; RB Jai’Den Thomas was 1st team All MWC in 2025 and returns this season.  One of the MWC favorites this fall;  Projected to win 7.5 by Vegas, tied for 2nd highest win total in the MWC. New QB is Jackson Arnold, who was a big recruit that failed to excel in the Auburn offense, like most QBs did under former HC Hugh Freeze. OL has 4 starters, 2 returning and 2 portal starters although new WR must emerge for what projects to be one of MWC best offenses under HC Mullen.  Defense led by DC Paul Guenther enters his second season with Lucas Conti as the only established interior anchor; replaces a lot but there's a lot of talent on this unit.
team_notes.utep: Tier 4 MWC Team: UTEP has been a fairly bad FBS program for a while now; although HC Scotty Walden is a high energy guy the results have not matched that enthusiasm. New starting QB EJ Colson fro FCS Incarnate Word was productive and mobile and gives the offense new life under new OC Joe Pappalardo; RB/WR are new and relying on portal additions; OL has 2 with starting experience from former schools, both recent portal additions.  A rebuild of this offense was needed, but there's a lot of projecting players stepping into roles they haven't proven they can perform at.  Defense is the better unit; DL looks solid with good experience in front of LB Jayden Wilson who is the top performer returning;  Secondary looks like strength of the team with guys like CB Justin Content & Safety Xavier Smith.
team_notes.wyoming: Tier 3 MWC Team: Play at high elevation & Laramie is a tricky place for teams to play; HC Jay Sawvel jsut 7-17 into his 3rd season.  Phil Steele named  12 Cowboys to his preseason All-Mountain West teams led by first-teamers Samuel Harris at RB, Ethan Stuhlsatz at LB. New QB Tyler Hughes from FCS William & Mary looks impressive and mobile, had a good spring where he won starting job.  RB looks solid with Samuel Harris off a productive freshman season and some good portal adds behind an OL that returns 2 starters and added 2 FCS starters in the transfer portal.  If new WR emerge this offense could take a jump up.  Defense usually better unit for Cowboys but must replace most from last season. They are relying on depth and a good number of FCS portal adds to fill new roles, CB Desman Hearns is the top performer returning to DC Aaron Bohl's unit.
